title: Stage 04d — Migration Safety
description: Conditional safety review for changes touching the data layer (schema files, migration directories, ALTER/CREATE/DROP TABLE DDL). Fires when stage-04a's pre-review heuristic sets migration_safety_required: true. Captures backfill plan, dual-write strategy, rollback plan. Has veto power — an unsafe migration halts the pipeline regardless of any other approval.

migration_files.description: Paths in this diff that the migration-heuristic flagged. Includes both path-matched files (migration dirs, schema files) and content-matched files (any file containing ALTER/CREATE/DROP TABLE DDL fragments).

schema_changes_summary.description: Plain-English description of what the migration changes — added column, dropped index, renamed table, etc. One paragraph max. The audit-facing 'what is this migration doing' answer.

breaking_change.description: Does this migration require coordination with currently-running code? Dropping a column, narrowing a type, adding NOT NULL without default, renaming a column without a view alias — all breaking. Adding a nullable column or a new table is non-breaking.

backfill_required.description: Does existing data need to be transformed to fit the new schema? Adding a NOT NULL column to a populated table, computing a derived field from existing rows, normalizing data into a new lookup table — all require backfill.

backfill_strategy.description: If backfill_required is true: HOW will the backfill run? Online (batched job) vs. offline (downtime window). Batch size + rate limit. Idempotency mechanism. Verification step. Concrete commands. If backfill_required is false: this field may be empty.

dual_write_required.description: Will writes need to go to BOTH old and new schemas during a transition window? Common when renaming a column, moving data to a new table, or sharding. The dual-write window lets you roll forward AND back without data loss.

dual_write_strategy.description: If dual_write_required is true: WHICH writes hit both, how long the window lasts, how the switchover happens, how the read path resolves conflicts. If false: may be empty.

rollback_plan.description: REQUIRED, non-empty. Concrete steps to roll the migration back. 'Run db/migrations/0042_rollback.sql' is acceptable; 'revert the PR' is NOT (because the column may already have been dropped). For irreversible migrations (e.g. DROP TABLE with data loss), the plan describes the recovery procedure from backups + the acceptable data-loss window. An empty rollback_plan auto-vetoes the migration.

rollback_tested.description: Was the rollback actually exercised — staging environment, throwaway database, dry-run script? Untested rollbacks frequently don't work when needed; this field separates documented from verified. A tested rollback is what graduates an emergency call from 'maybe' to 'definitely.'

migration_approved.description: Migration role's overall approval. PASS gates require this true AND veto false AND non-empty rollback_plan.

veto.description: Veto flag — when true, the pipeline halts at this stage regardless of any other approval. Auto-set true when rollback_plan is empty, rollback_tested is false on a breaking_change, or backfill_strategy is empty when backfill_required is true. Peer-review approvals do NOT override a veto; the migration role must personally re-review the fix.

triggering_conditions.description: Why this stage fired — the conditions in stage-04a that triggered the migration-safety review. Auditable trace.
